_status: partially populated (2026-07-07 second + third research pass) - see data-requirements.md §17/§18 and ISSUES.md ISS-9/ISS-3

usefulLifeYears.note: Companies Act Schedule II explicitly names 'Cat-scan' machines at 13 years, the diagnostic-scan category. See data-requirements.md §12.4/§14.

salvageValuePercentage.note: Companies Act Schedule II caps residual value at 5% of original cost unless separately justified. See data-requirements.md §18.2 for the citation-correction caveat (same as MRI).

installationAndAncillaryCostPercentage.note: NEIGRIHMS 128-slice CT tender requires a minimum 10% of product cost allocated to installation/commissioning/testing -- a tender-mandated bid-allocation floor, not measured actual average spend. See data-requirements.md §18.3. Normalized to {low,typical,high} 2026-07-13 (PBA-1) for cross-equipment schema consistency -- a single reported figure, not a real range, so low=typical=high.

warrantyYears.note: Karnataka KKRDB tender specifies 3-year warranty; NEIGRIHMS 128-slice CT tender specifies 5-year warranty + 5yr CMC. Genuine tender-to-tender variation. See data-requirements.md §18.1.

cmcYears.note: Follows the 5-year warranty in the NEIGRIHMS tender. See data-requirements.md §18.4. Normalized to {low,typical,high} 2026-07-13 (PBA-1) -- a single reported figure, not a real range, so low=typical=high.

amcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
amcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Labour-only AMC. Generic tender clause (SCTIMST) applied identically across all 5 equipment types -- not CT-specific. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

cmcAnnualCostPercentage.unit: percent of equipment cost per year
cmcAnnualCostPercentage.note: Generic tender-ceiling range (GeM + SCTIMST caps), not CT-specific. Unlike MRI, no dedicated real-cost study was found for CT to compare against. See data-requirements.md §18.4.

_usagePerDayRange.note: No genuine Indian standalone-CT utilization study found. low=15 is scans/day/scanner derived from an AIIMS PET/CT cost analysis (PET/CT throughput is not directly comparable to standard CT). high=30 is an unsourced 'engineering guide' estimate. Left as a range, not a single typical value, given the weak sourcing. A third research pass (2026-07-07) found a similar-looking 15-30 range from IPHS district-hospital guidelines (S57), but that figure is a COMBINED X-ray+CT+ultrasound workload norm, not CT-specific -- weaker, not stronger, than the existing PET/CT-derived figure, so no change was made here. See data-requirements.md §18.7. Standalone CT utilization remains a genuine open gap.

billedTariffPerUse.note: CT head/brain (plain) CGHS reimbursement ceiling -- a government-scheme floor, NOT a private cash tariff. Other CT procedures price very differently: with contrast ~1870/2200, chest/HRCT ~1700/2000, whole abdomen with contrast ~4399/5175. This field anchors to plain head/brain as the baseline only. Stale (2013) private-hospital data point for context (S31): CT head ~INR 900, CT chest/abdomen ~INR 1200 -- 13 years old, do not use as a current default.

launchDelayMonths.note: Delivery-to-first-scan once site is ready is only 3-5 days (S33); the dominant driver is AERB radiation approval, qualitatively described as 'a few weeks to a couple of months' with no exact source figure (S34, approximated here as 3-6 weeks). Does NOT include civil/room-shielding construction time itself, which no source quantified -- real-world total launch delay is likely higher than this range alone.
